exercises.title: DIGIT BLEED
exercises.brief: Bleed an integer down one digit at a time and sum the drops.
promptLines: Define **sumDigits(n)** returning the sum of the decimal digits of a non-negative integer `n` — **recursively**, no loops. Base case: a single digit (`n < 10`) returns itself.
starterLines: // TODO: if n < 10 return n; else (n % 10) + sumDigits(Math.floor(n / 10))

tests.name: single digit
tests.code: assertEqual(sumDigits(7), 7, 'one digit is its own sum');

exercises.lore: Peel one digit off the wire, let the rest bleed out below.

exercises.title: BACKMASK
exercises.brief: Spin the signal backwards, one character at a time.
promptLines: Define **reverseStr(s)** returning the string reversed — **recursively**, no loops or `.reverse()`. Base case: the empty string returns `''`.
starterLines: // TODO: if empty return ''; else reverseStr(rest) + first char
exercises.lore: Run the tape backwards and the message reads true.

exercises.title: ECHO LOOP
exercises.brief: Stack the same fragment n deep without a single loop.
promptLines: Define **repeatStr(s, n)** returning `s` concatenated `n` times — **recursively**, no loops or `.repeat()`. Base case: `n === 0` returns `''`.
starterLines: // TODO: if n === 0 return ''; else s + repeatStr(s, n - 1)

tests.name: single copy
tests.code: assertEqual(repeatStr('hi', 1), 'hi', 'one copy is the string itself');

exercises.hint: Base: n === 0 -> ''. Else s + repeatStr(s, n - 1).
exercises.lore: Each call leaves an echo, stacked until the count hits zero.

exercises.title: MIRROR PROBE
exercises.brief: Probe a string from both ends, collapsing toward the middle.
promptLines: Define **isPalindrome(s)** returning `true` if `s` reads the same forwards and backwards — **recursively**, no loops or `.reverse()`. Compare the two ends, then recurse on the middle. Base case: length `<= 1` is `true`.
starterLines: // TODO: if length <= 1 return true; if ends differ return false; else recurse on the middle

tests.name: hello -> false
tests.code: assert(isPalindrome('hello') === false, 'hello is not a palindrome');

tests.name: empty -> true
tests.code: assert(isPalindrome('') === true, 'base case empty is true');

tests.name: single char
tests.code: assert(isPalindrome('a') === true, 'one char is a palindrome');

exercises.hint: Base: length <= 1 -> true. Else if s[0] !== last char -> false; else isPalindrome(s.slice(1, -1)).
exercises.lore: Touch both ends to the mirror; if they match, fall inward.

exercises.title: LAUNCH SEQUENCE
exercises.brief: Build the launch sequence n..1 by stacking the descent.
promptLines: Define **countDownArr(n)** returning an array counting down from `n` to `1` — **recursively**, no loops. Base case: `n <= 0` returns `[]`.
exercises.lore: Each call drops a rung; the floor of the descent is the empty array.
